title: Crispy Portuguese Lentil Fritters
description: A traditional Portuguese dish featuring crispy lentil fritters, flavored with herbs and spices, perfect as a snack or side dish. This low-fat-friendly recipe is a nostalgic treat that combines the comfort of lentils with the crunch of a fried exterior.
instructions: In a large bowl, soak the lentils in water for at least 4 hours or overnight. Drain and set aside.
In a pan, heat 2 tablespoons of olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked lentils, onion mixture, chopped parsley, chopped cilantro, paprika, salt, and black pepper. Mix well.
Add the whole wheat flour and beaten egg to the bowl, mixing until a thick batter forms.
Using your hands, shape the batter into small patties, about 1 1/2 inches in diameter and 1/2 inch thick.
Heat about 1/2 inch of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until it reaches 350°F.
Carefully place the lentil patties in the hot oil, being careful not to overcrowd the skillet.
Fry the lentil fritters for about 3-4 minutes on each side, until crispy and golden brown.
Remove the fritters from the oil with a slotted spoon and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the crispy Portuguese lentil fritters warm, garnished with additional parsley and cilantro if desired.
